title: Decaloba rohrii
intro: Decaloba rohrii, also known as Rohia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its unique properties and medicinal applications. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Decaloba rohrii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Decaloba rohrii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, from fever and inflammation to skin conditions and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Decaloba rohrii belongs to the family Passifloraceae and is a type of flowering plant. It is a species of passionflower, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant is native to South America and is often classified as an annual or perennial vine. Within the Passifloraceae family, Decaloba rohrii is part of the genus Decaloba. The classification of the plant is further refined by its specific characteristics and growth habits.
uses: Decaloba rohrii has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world. Its roots and leaves are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections. Decaloba rohrii is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.
